preview.summary: Two of the group's outsiders meet in a potentially decisive Group H clash, with debutants Cape Verde facing a Saudi Arabia side under a new coach. Both will view this as their most winnable fixture against Spain and Uruguay elsewhere in the group.
preview.homeForm: Debutants Cape Verde came through CAF qualifying unbeaten and warmed up by beating Serbia 3-0, sitting 69th in the rankings.
preview.awayForm: Saudi Arabia, 62nd in the world, appointed Georgios Donis weeks before the tournament after sacking Herve Renard.
preview.homeKey: Laros Duarte, unpredictable midfielder with a finishing instinct.
preview.awayKey: Salem Al-Dawsari, captain and reigning AFC Player of the Year.
storylines: Cape Verde's first ever World Cup appearance
Saudi Arabia field a squad built almost entirely on domestic players
The two nations have never met before
preview.h2h: There is no previous meeting between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia.
